is [Central European Time](/wiki/Central_European_Time "Central European Time") (*Mitteleuropäische Zeit*, *MEZ*; [UTC+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00")) and [Central European Summer Time](/wiki/Central_European_Summer_Time "Central European Summer Time") (*Mitteleuropäische Sommerzeit*, *MESZ*; [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00")). [Daylight saving time](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time") is observed from the last Sunday in March (02:00 CET) to the last Sunday in October (03:00 CEST). The doubled hour during the switch back to standard time is named 2A (02:00 to 03:00 CEST) and 2B (02:00 to 03:00 CET).
Lennox, Jonathan (2008-02-11). ["Re: FW: FW: Corrections to historic German timezone information"](http://article.gmane.org/gmane.comp.time.tz/2074/match=east+germany). gmane.comp.time.tz.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8e/Time_zones_of_the_Greater_Europe.svg/330px-Time_zones_of_the_Greater_Europe.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Time_zones_of_the_Greater_Europe.svg)

[Time in Europe](/wiki/Time_in_Europe "Time in Europe"):

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Light Blue | [Western European Time](/wiki/Western_European_Time "Western European Time") / [Greenwich Mean Time](/wiki/Greenwich_Mean_Time "Greenwich Mean Time") ([UTC](/wiki/UTC%2B00:00 "UTC+00:00")) |
| Blue | [Western European Time](/wiki/Western_European_Time "Western European Time") / [Greenwich Mean Time](/wiki/Greenwich_Mean_Time "Greenwich Mean Time") ([UTC](/wiki/UTC%2B00:00 "UTC+00:00")) |
| [Western European Summer Time](/wiki/Western_European_Summer_Time "Western European Summer Time") / [British Summer Time](/wiki/British_Summer_Time "British Summer Time") / [Irish Standard Time](/wiki/Time_in_the_Republic_of_Ireland "Time in the Republic of Ireland") ([UTC+1](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00")) |
| Red | [Central European Time](/wiki/Central_European_Time "Central European Time") ([UTC+1](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00")) |
| [Central European Summer Time](/wiki/Central_European_Summer_Time "Central European Summer Time") ([UTC+2](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00")) |
| Yellow | [Eastern European Time](/wiki/Eastern_European_Time "Eastern European Time") / [Kaliningrad Time](/wiki/Kaliningrad_Time "Kaliningrad Time") ([UTC+2](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00")) |
| Ochre | [Eastern European Time](/wiki/Eastern_European_Time "Eastern European Time") ([UTC+2](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00")) |
| [Eastern European Summer Time](/wiki/Eastern_European_Summer_Time "Eastern European Summer Time") ([UTC+3](/wiki/UTC%2B03:00 "UTC+03:00")) |
| Green | [Moscow Time](/wiki/Moscow_Time "Moscow Time") / [Turkey Time](/wiki/Time_in_Turkey "Time in Turkey") ([UTC+3](/wiki/UTC%2B03:00 "UTC+03:00")) |
| Turquoise | [Armenia Time](/wiki/Armenia_Time "Armenia Time") / [Azerbaijan Time](/wiki/Azerbaijan_Time "Azerbaijan Time") / [Georgia Time](/wiki/Georgia_Time "Georgia Time") / [Samara Time](/wiki/Samara_Time "Samara Time") ([UTC+4](/wiki/UTC%2B04:00 "UTC+04:00")) |

▉▉▉▉ Pale hues: Standard time observed all year   
   ▉▉▉   Dark hues: [Daylight saving time](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time")

The **[time zone](/wiki/Time_zone "Time zone") in [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany")** is [Central European Time](/wiki/Central_European_Time "Central European Time") (*Mitteleuropäische Zeit*, *MEZ*; [UTC+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00")) and [Central European Summer Time](/wiki/Central_European_Summer_Time "Central European Summer Time") (*Mitteleuropäische Sommerzeit*, *MESZ*; [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00")). [Daylight saving time](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time") is observed from the last Sunday in March (02:00 CET) to the last Sunday in October (03:00 CEST). The doubled hour during the switch back to standard time is named 2A (02:00 to 03:00 CEST) and 2B (02:00 to 03:00 CET).

## IANA time zone database

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Time_in_Germany&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: IANA time zone database")]

The [IANA time zone database](/wiki/IANA_time_zone_database "IANA time zone database") contains two zones for Germany, "Europe/Berlin" and "Europe/Busingen", although in 1945, the [Trizone](/wiki/Trizone "Trizone") did not follow [Berlin](/wiki/Berlin "Berlin")'s switch to midsummer time.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

[Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany") had been politically divided into [East Germany](/wiki/East_Germany "East Germany") and [West Germany](/wiki/West_Germany "West Germany") at and after the start of the [Unix epoch](/wiki/Unix_time "Unix time"), which is the date from which the tz database wants to record correct information. The database aims to include at least one zone for every [ISO 3166-1](/wiki/ISO_3166-1 "ISO 3166-1") alpha-2 country code. This list was first issued in 1997, after the reunification of Germany in 1990. Hence only the unified Germany is listed.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

The zone Europe/Busingen was created in the 2013a release of the tz database,[[2]](#cite_note-2) because since the [Unix time](/wiki/Unix_time "Unix time") epoch in 1970, [Büsingen](/wiki/B%C3%BCsingen_am_Hochrhein "Büsingen am Hochrhein") has shared clocks with [Zurich](/wiki/Zurich "Zurich").[[3]](#cite_note-3) Büsingen did not observe DST in 1980 like the rest of West Germany, but did so from 1981 after [Switzerland](/wiki/Switzerland "Switzerland") adopted DST. Büsingen is a German exclave located in Switzerland. That's why Swiss regulations apply in some cases.

| [c.c.](/wiki/ISO_3166_2-character_country_code "ISO 3166 2-character country code") | Coordinates | [Timezone name](/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones "List of tz database time zones") | Comments | [UTC offset](/wiki/UTC_offset "UTC offset") ([Std.](/wiki/Standard_time "Standard time")) | UTC offset ([DST](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time")) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [DE](/wiki/ISO_3166-1:DE "ISO 3166-1:DE") | +5230+01322 | Europe/Berlin | most of Germany | [+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00") | [+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00") |
| [DE](/wiki/ISO_3166-1:DE "ISO 3166-1:DE") | +4742+00841 | Europe/Busingen | Busingen | [+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00") | [+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00") |

## Daylight saving time

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Time_in_Germany&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Daylight saving time")]

[Daylight saving time](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time") was first introduced during [World War I](/wiki/World_War_I "World War I") by the [German Empire](/wiki/German_Empire "German Empire") in the years 1916 to 1918. After the end of the war and the proclamation of the [Weimar Republic](/wiki/Weimar_Republic "Weimar Republic") in November 1918, daylight saving time ceased to be observed. It was used again 1940–1949 (from 1945 differently in the West and East) and introduced again from 1980. West and East Germany had the same time and DST from 1950 until unification. In 1996, daylight saving time was harmonised throughout the [European Union](/wiki/European_Union "European Union") by Directive 2000/84/EC, which moved the end of DST to the last Sunday in October.

In 1980 the exclave [Büsingen](/wiki/B%C3%BCsingen_am_Hochrhein "Büsingen am Hochrhein") did not use DST in order to keep to the [same time as Switzerland](/wiki/Time_in_Switzerland "Time in Switzerland").[[4]](#cite_note-4)
